title: UN Human Rights Chief Calls for Independent Investigations into ICE Custody Deaths
excerpt: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk formally called for independent investigations into all deaths in ICE custody, citing 19 deaths in the first five months of 2026 — more than double the same period the prior year — and expressing alarm at solitary confinement and inadequate healthcare.
content:
On June 26, 2026, UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk formally called for prompt, independent, impartial and effective investigations into all deaths in ICE custody and urged stronger independent oversight of the U.S. immigration detention system.

  • According to U.S. government official data, 18 people died in ICE detention in the first five months of 2026, with an additional death reported in early June, bringing the total to 19. This is more than double the eight deaths in the same period the prior year.
  • ICE currently holds over 60,000 people in detention, with plans to expand capacity to 90,000 by the end of 2026.
  • Türk expressed alarm over the reported use of solitary confinement, which he said could amount to "cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment" when prolonged or indefinite, in violation of international human rights law.
  • Five of the officially reported deaths in 2026 were classified as suicides. Türk raised concerns about whether some deaths could have been prevented given "the lack of transparency surrounding the circumstances of deaths" and disruptions during detainee transfers.
  • Detainees are frequently reported to face inadequate healthcare and food, and exposure to disease in overcrowded facilities, Türk said.

"The High Commissioner calls for prompt, independent, impartial and effective investigations into all deaths in ICE custody," the OHCHR spokesperson said. "Those responsible for violations of the law must be held to account."
mediaPlaceholder: UN press briefing footage of OHCHR spokesperson Marta Hurtado delivering UN High Commissioner Türk's statement on ICE custody deaths
imageAlt: UN Human Rights Chief Calls for Independent Investigations into ICE Custody Deaths
